_purpose: Robel's hand-authored golden pairs (Task 22). Real-world confusables with the
outcome YOU expect from match(): 'same' person, 'different' people, or 'review'
(the analyst zone strictly between the two thresholds).
These pairs ARE the human baseline, so they ship needs_human=false in pairs.json.
The agent never authors a pair here -- same firewall as lexicon entries
An empty 'pairs' list is valid: it means 'generated corpus only'.
_how_to_use: 1. Add objects to 'pairs' below (keys: a, b, expected, and an optional note).
(never hand-edit tests/golden/pairs.json -- the --check gate compares it
A pair the engine currently fails is NOT dropped: it is recorded with
"known_fail": true in pairs.json and the suite asserts it KEEPS failing, so
fixing it later forces a conscious regeneration. That is the honest record of
an engine limit; the 0.2.0 target for known_fail is 0.

_fields.a: one spelling (Latin or fidel; fidel is fine -- the matcher normalizes)
_fields.b: the other spelling; must differ from 'a'
_fields.note: optional, free text -- appears verbatim in the pair's 'source' field in pairs.json

_example_shape_do_not_uncomment.note: <why you say so -- e.g. two distinct names an OCR pass confuses>

_collides_with_generated: If your pair repeats a generated pair's two spellings, YOURS WINS: the curated
pair replaces it (expected value included), and pairs.json records the count

pairs.note: swapped roles: given and patronym inverted
swapped roles: given and patronym inverted
missing patronym: a skipped generation, typically rejected in strict KYC
missing patronym: a skipped generation, typically rejected in strict KYC
variant interaction within a multi-token name: variant in the given name
variant interaction within a multi-token name: variant in the given name
definitively different: shared given name, different patronym
definitively different: sharing a prefix does not make it the same name
